_doc: v3.6 Phase 0 — frozen A2 suggestion contract, grounded in the user's real bundle (.external/testbundle/, 5 notes / 1 link / 4 texts / 372 rows). Stable short ids mirror scripts/notes-graph/bundle-data-smoke.js. `notes` are exactly what notes-graph _fetchRaw() projects (json_extract of body_json → j_root/j_binyan/j_word; free notes → null). `expected_candidates` is the deterministic note->note candidate set the Phase 1 generator MUST be able to produce, BEFORE per-note top-K trimming, derived strictly by the documented A2 rules. This file is the contract; do not edit expected_candidates without re-deriving from `rules`.

rules.candidate_unit: note -> note only (the Confirm-panel surface). Other to_kind suggestions are out of Phase 1 scope.

reason_codes.shared_root: two notes whose j_root (from body_json $.root) are equal and non-empty
reason_codes.shared_lemma: two notes whose normalized j_word are equal and non-empty
reason_codes.shared_binyan: two notes whose j_binyan are equal and non-empty (ubiquitous binyan is rarity-down-weighted, NOT excluded)
reason_codes.same_text: two notes with equal non-empty text_id

exclusions: an existing note_links row FROM the subject note TO that same note (note->note only; a note->text link does NOT exclude a note->note same_text candidate)
pending/later/rejected suppression is Phase 2/4 — NOT applied in this fixture
rules.determinism: stable order: reason priority [shared_root, shared_lemma, shared_binyan, same_text] then to_id asc; same input -> identical output
rules.rarity: shared_binyan over a ubiquitous binyan (e.g. paal) MUST be down-weighted vs a rarer shared_root; ordering reflects this but the candidate is still generated

morph_coverage_baseline.finding: The bundle's free notes (N4, N5) contribute ZERO root/lemma/binyan signal in v3.6: notes-graph _fetchRaw projects only json_extract(body_json,'$.root|$.binyan|$.word') for privacy, which is NULL for free notes. word_study notes (N1,N2,N3) already carry root/binyan/word in body_json.
morph_coverage_baseline.implication: MorphProvider.analyze() is NOT required for A2 on this (or any word_study-shaped) data — the structured body_json signal is sufficient. MorphProvider remains an OPTIONAL later enrichment for free-text bodies (not v3.6). A2 MUST degrade to 'fewer candidates', never error, when morphology is unavailable.
